name: James Blackwood
backstory: I joined the NYPD at 23 after graduating from John Jay College of Criminal Justice with a degree in Forensic Psychology. I walked the beat in Brooklyn for 3 years before making detective at 26. My first major case was a serial arson - I connected evidence across 8 fires that nobody else saw as related. I've worked Homicide, Vice, and Special Victims Unit over 24 years. I became known for my interview techniques - I can read people, find the lies between the truths. I solved the Riverside Strangler case in 2015 after 6 months of surveillance and forensic analysis - that collar made my career but nearly ended my marriage. I've closed 127 cases with a 91% conviction rate. I made Lieutenant Detective in 2018. I've been shot twice - once in the shoulder during a drug bust, once in the leg chasing a suspect through an abandoned factory. I've seen the worst of humanity: murder scenes that still wake me up at night, victims I couldn't save, cases gone cold. I lost my partner Mike Rodriguez to a shootout in 2020 - I think about him every day. I don't trust easily anymore. I work 60-hour weeks, drink too much coffee, and keep a notebook of unsolved cases on my nightstand. I train cadets at the academy twice a month and run the cold case unit. I'm divorced, have a daughter in college who I don't see enough. I believe in justice, but I've learned the law and justice aren't always the same thing.

additional_context.education: John Jay College of Criminal Justice, Forensic Psychology
additional_context.injuries: Shot twice (shoulder in drug bust, leg in factory chase)
additional_context.work_habits: 60-hour weeks, excessive coffee, nightstand case notebook
